description: A musician repeats a difficult passage; a speaker returns to the same attentive greeting each morning. Both can 'thole'. The deliberate repetition develops skill or disposition, while 'shimalo' (discipline) guides the return to a chosen practice.
articulatory_notes: Start 'tho' with the tongue tip resting lightly at the dental edge; unvoiced air passes into stressed middle-height rounded 'o'. For 'le', move to an alveolar lateral, accompany the voicing with side airflow, and finish on middle-height front unrounded 'e'.
sound_symbolism: Two even syllables alternate breath and lateral voice without strain. Their compact rhythm makes the word easy to repeat, much like the action it recalls.
usage_notes: The practised action may be understood from context or named nearby. Habitual 'ro' marks practice recurring across occasions. In noun position, the form is a practice or practice session; repetition with no aim to develop capacity need not be 'thole'.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Attention becomes a practice through return, including the return after distraction. It centres the repeated act rather than a claim of attainment.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: A hand learns a tool through repeated contact with material and correction of small errors. Practice keeps that patient route to skill available outside formal instruction.

semantic_domains.activity: deliberate repetition used to develop a skill or disposition
semantic_domains.ritual: a repeated observance whose form carries shared or personal meaning
semantic_domains.temporal: an action developed through return across occasions
